memory.summary: I have a free afternoon with low stress, but I still have significant sleep debt and only barely started my term project due Thursday.
I spent half an hour on a trivial chore with no meaningful progress, despite having time and low stress.
Spending the afternoon on trivial tasks is delaying my term project, which still needs significant work before Thursday.
Working on the term project now directly reduces future stress and crunch time, whereas procrastinating only compounds my anxiety.
I spent another 30 minutes on a trivial chore, wasting time while my term project deadline looms closer.
I am continuing to waste time on trivial chores despite knowing this delays my term project and increases stress.
I just made quick progress on a trivial chore, but my memory confirms I keep delaying the term project despite knowing it increases stress.
I spent 30 minutes on a trivial task, gaining minimal progress while my stress rises and the term project deadline approaches.
I spent 30 minutes on a trivial task, gaining minimal progress while my stress rises and the term project deadline approaches.
I spent another 30 minutes on a trivial task, gaining minimal progress while my stress rises and the term project deadline approaches.
I have spent over an hour on a trivial task with minimal progress, and I still cannot finish it before the deadline in under an hour.
Spending over an hour on this trivial task yields negligible progress while my stress rises and the project deadline looms.

goals.description: Complete the trivial easy chore (instant win) to clear a pending task.

goals.rationale: The project is due in 3 days with only ~24% completion and 'soft' hardness; immediate action is required to prevent falling behind before the Thursday deadline.
